name: 32 · xArm Trajectory Feedback Stream (ROS)
description: Streams the structured feedback of an xArm 6 FollowJointTrajectory action through a streaming channel (docs/25). Same `send_action_goal` to `/xarm6_traj_controller/follow_joint_trajectory` (`control_msgs/action/FollowJointTrajectory`) as demo 31, but the `move` node declares a Control/Out channel `feedback`: as the ros2_control controller follows the trajectory it publishes nested `FollowJointTrajectory_Feedback` frames (`{ header, joint_names, desired, actual, error }`) that the ROS backend dedups and streams as `item` control tokens into the channel, then a `close` carrying the count. The consumer edge to `fold` carries `join: gather`, so the compiler's per-channel gather barrier parks the gathered collection `{ output: [...] }` on the channel's gathered place; the downstream `fold` reducer (a plain Python AutomatedStep) drains it off `input.output`, counts the distinct frames and surfaces the first one verbatim. No per-feedback token enters the marking (2 net firings for the whole stream). End maps `feedback_count = {{ fold.feedback_count }}` and `sample_frame = {{ fold.sample_frame }}`, proving rich, nested ROS action feedback survives the channel round-trip without depending on its inner field names. LIVE ONLY: requires `just dev xarm-up` to build the xArm 6 + rosbridge container and enroll an `xarm_fleet` runner advertising `ros.robot_model == xarm6`.
